---
title: Extended non-negative real line and limit
author: JK
date: '2020-12-29'
slug: []
categories:
  - Math
tags:
  - Analysis
  - Limit
subtitle: ''
summary: ''
authors: []
lastmod: '2020-12-29T07:39:56+09:00'
featured: no
draft: true
image:
  caption: ''
  focal_point: ''
  preview_only: no
projects: []
---
This is a note of An introduction to measure theory, Terence Tao https://terrytao.wordpress.com/books/an-introduction-to-measure-theory/.
In the extended non-negative real line, an infinite series $\Sigma_{n=1}^{\infty} x_{n} \in [0, \infty]$ is always convergent as a limit of the partial sum $\Sigma_{n=1}^{N} x_{n}$.  

The Jordan measure is an infimum of the finite sum of element measure. The Lebegue measure is an infimum of the infinite sum of element measure. the questions are whether the infimum a kind of limit, and what is difference between the Lebegue measure and as a limit of Jordan measure.  

Jordan measure is not a limit of the partial sum but a limit of sequence $\lim_{n \rightarrow \infty} x_{n}$. The $n$ is a number of element measures. This is a traditinal thought of
